content:
FINAL DEVICE VERIFICATION BLOCK — CLOSE IVX IA SENIOR DEVELOPER AUDIT

The local code, automated tests, GitHub commit, Render deployment, production health, Metro runtime, and Expo tunnel are already reported as verified.

Now complete the remaining real-device verification end to end.

CURRENT EXPO SESSION:
exp://qlagr90-anonymous-8081.exp.direct

EXECUTE AND MONITOR NOW:

1. Keep Metro and the Expo tunnel running continuously.
2. Watch Metro logs, Expo connection logs, Render logs, Socket.IO logs, and owner-chat logs in real time while I test from my Android phone.
3. Do not regenerate the tunnel unless the current tunnel actually fails.
4. Do not mark the task complete until the physical-device test below is finished.

PHYSICAL ANDROID TEST

A. Open the Expo link in Expo Go.
B. Confirm the real IVX app renders, not:
   - “Something went wrong”
   - a blank screen
   - a placeholder
   - a Swift shell
   - an old cached build

C. Verify the landing page loads correctly.
D. Verify navigation works without crashes.
E. Open Owner Sign-In.
F. Sign in with the existing owner account.
G. Confirm full owner access loads.
H. Open IVX Owner AI chat.
I. Send this exact message:

DEVICE VERIFICATION TEST — reply with current server time and trace ID.

J. Confirm the message shows as delivered.
K. Confirm the AI returns a real response.
L. Confirm no raw stack trace, 401, 403, gateway error, or timeout appears.
M. Close Expo Go completely.
N. Reopen the same project.
O. Confirm owner session and conversation history persist.
P. Send a second message.
Q. Confirm no duplicate, missing, delayed, or out-of-order messages.
R. Verify keyboard does not cover the composer.
S. Verify scroll-to-latest works.
T. Verify typing/working state renders correctly.

WHILE I TEST, CAPTURE:

1. Expo device connection timestamp
2. Metro Android bundle result
3. Exact route opened
4. Owner-auth result
5. conversationId
6. userMessageId
7. assistantMessageId
8. AI provider
9. AI model
10. Provider HTTP status
11. Socket.IO connection result
12. Persistence result after reopen
13. Any exact error, file, and line if a step fails

FAILURE POLICY

If the device shows an error:

1. Stop the audit immediately.
2. Capture the exact Expo and Metro error.
3. Identify the root cause and exact file/line.
4. Fix it.
5. Run typecheck, build, and tests again.
6. Commit and push the fix.
7. Deploy if required.
8. Generate a fresh Expo tunnel only if necessary.
9. Repeat the complete physical-device test.

REQUIRED FINAL EVIDENCE

A. Android screenshot showing IVX rendered
B. Owner sign-in screenshot
C. Working IVX Owner AI conversation screenshot
D. conversationId
E. userMessageId
F. assistantMessageId
G. Persistence proof after app reopen
H. GitHub commit SHA
I. Render deployment ID
J. Production /health HTTP 200
K. Exact tunnel URL used
L. Remaining blockers, if any

FINAL STATUS MUST BE EXACTLY ONE:

IVX_IA_SENIOR_DEVELOPER_DEVICE_VERIFIED_COMPLETE

or

IVX_IA_DEVICE_TEST_FAILED_WITH_EXACT_ERROR

Do not provide another general status report. Stay live, observe the device session, fix any failure, and close the audit with real device evidence.
